description_long: This is a large, military-style cargo airplane with a dark green exterior, featuring a broad fuselage and a high-mounted straight wing configuration. The aircraft is equipped with four prominent turboprop engines, each with visible propellers mounted on the wings. The nose is rounded and slightly pointed, with cockpit windows set forward and above the main fuselage. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer in a conventional arrangement. The landing gear appears to be fixed and robust, suitable for heavy loads and rougher surfaces. The overall design suggests a utilitarian aircraft built for transport and durability.
description: A large, dark green military cargo airplane with four turboprop engines, high-mounted straight wings, and a conventional tail. The design is robust and utilitarian, with a forward cockpit and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad wingspan, four propeller engines, and the rounded nose with cockpit windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the high-wing configuration, engine placement, and the length of the fuselage with visible landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the single vertical stabilizer, horizontal stabilizer, and the symmetry of the wings and engines.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing similar features as view 1, with clear visibility of the cockpit and engine arrangement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main units and a nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
